verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific single-pass transmembrane protein (Ig-like fold) that resides in the acrosomal membrane of non-capacitated sperm and relocates to the entire sperm plasma membrane surface after the acrosomal reaction. On acrosome-reacted sperm the extracellular domain is fully exposed and mediates binding to oocyte JUNO (Folr4) — this is the essential sperm-egg fusion event. Thus the protein body is surface-accessible on the outer leaflet, but only in the specific cellular context of acrosome-reacted (capacitated/activated) sperm. This qualifies as tissue_restricted_surface / cell-state-induced surface expression on a specialized cell type.
